ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #19, REPORT
Off Calendar Aug-19-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Natacha Ivacheff-Kolb for Carolyn Collins and Debra Dolch.

The Court stated that it did not have any issue with the determination of the Trustee and Counsel that it should be gross income nor any issue with the commissions to the Trustees. The Court also stated that the Attorney's fees were quite high. Ms. Ivacheff-Kolb presented oral argument to support the Attorney's fee request stating that time was spent addressing the Examiner's notes, filing two amended petitions, that there was no objection to the fees by the Client and that she believed the fees were reasonable. The Court took the matter under submission stating that there would be a reduction in fees as the hourly rate was high.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
